syllabus.atAGlance: Learn core AI concepts and Generative AI while exploring ethical considerations to kickstart your AI career.
syllabus.intro: This Artificial Intelligence Fundamentals course teaches you the skills required to move into various AI roles. You will gain foundational, job-ready knowledge along with practical exposure and an industry-recognized credential.
youWillLearn: Job-ready skills in just 3 weeks with hands-on experience
Core concepts of Artificial Intelligence
How AI applications and use cases transform work and life
The impact of AI on businesses and careers
Issues, limitations, and ethical concerns surrounding AI
overview: Artificial Intelligence is seamlessly integrated into our daily lives and professional environments. This course introduces key AI terminologies, concepts, and applications to help you launch or transition into an AI-driven career.

You will explore core AI concepts including machine learning, neural networks, and deep learning. The course also introduces Generative AI models such as Large Language Models (LLMs) and their capabilities.

You will examine AI applications across NLP, computer vision, robotics, and business use cases. Ethical considerations, governance, and responsible AI usage are also covered. Hands-on labs and a final project provide practical experience.

modules.title: Module 1: Introduction and Applications of AI
topics: Generative AI and its Use Cases
AI in Daily Life and Smart Assistants
Generative AI Tools and Hands-on Labs

modules.title: Module 2: AI Concepts, Terminology, and Domains
topics: Generative AI Models and Large Language Models
Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision
AI in Self-Driving Cars, Cloud, Edge, and IoT

modules.title: Module 3: Business and Career Transformation Through AI
topics: Using AI Tools to Transform Your Work

modules.title: Module 4: Issues, Concerns, and Ethical Considerations
topics: Responsible and Ethical Use of AI
Final Project: Transforming Organizational Functions with AI

syllabus.prerequisites: This is a beginner-level course. No prior knowledge of Artificial Intelligence or programming is required.

syllabus.atAGlance: Dive into the new and exciting world of Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLM). Understand the capabilities of generative AI and its use cases in the real world. Identify the applications of generative AI in different sectors and industries. Explore common generative AI models and tools for text, code, image, audio, and video generation. This course is designed for everyone, including professionals, executives, students, and enthusiasts, interested in learning about generative AI and leveraging its capabilities in their work and lives. This course is your first step toward understanding the capabilities of generative AI, powered by different models, including large language models (LLMs).
syllabus.intro: In this course, you will learn about the fundamentals and evolution of generative AI. You will explore the capabilities of generative AI in different domains, including text, image, audio, video, virtual worlds, code, and data. You will understand the applications of generative AI across different sectors and industries. You will learn about the capabilities and features of common generative AI models and tools, such as GPT, DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, and Synthesia. Hands-on labs, included in the course, provide an opportunity to explore the use cases of generative AI through IBM Generative AI Classroom and popular tools like ChatGPT. You will also hear from the practitioners about the capabilities, applications, and tools of generative AI.
youWillLearn: Practical Generative AI skills to become job-ready in a short time
Foundational concepts of Generative AI and how models generate text, images, and code
Real-world Generative AI use cases across industries and daily work
How Generative AI is reshaping careers, productivity, and business models
Responsible use, limitations, and ethical challenges of Generative AI
overview: Artificial Intelligence is seamlessly integrated into our daily lives and professional environments. This course introduces key AI terminologies, concepts, and applications to help you launch or transition into an AI-driven career.

You will explore core AI concepts including machine learning, neural networks, and deep learning. The course also introduces Generative AI models such as Large Language Models (LLMs) and their capabilities.

You will examine AI applications across NLP, computer vision, robotics, and business use cases. Ethical considerations, governance, and responsible AI usage are also covered. Hands-on labs and a final project provide practical experience.

modules.title: Module 1: Introduction and Applications of AI
topics: Generative AI and its Use Cases
AI in Daily Life and Smart Assistants
Generative AI Tools and Hands-on Labs

modules.title: Module 2: AI Concepts, Terminology, and Domains
topics: Generative AI Models and Large Language Models
Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision
AI in Self-Driving Cars, Cloud, Edge, and IoT

modules.title: Module 3: Business and Career Transformation Through AI
topics: Using AI Tools to Transform Your Work

modules.title: Module 4: Issues, Concerns, and Ethical Considerations
topics: Responsible and Ethical Use of AI
Final Project: Transforming Organizational Functions with AI

syllabus.prerequisites: This is a beginner-level course. No prior knowledge of Artificial Intelligence or programming is required.

machinelearningusingpython.name: Machine Learning using Python
machinelearningusingpython.mainName: IBM Machine Learning using Python Certification

syllabus.atAGlance: Machine Learning is the foundation of Data Science and Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Python is the language of choice. Get started with machine learning and Python by enrolling in this hands-on course designed to build strong foundational ML skills.
syllabus.intro: This Machine Learning with Python course introduces the fundamentals of machine learning using an approachable and widely used programming language. You will learn the differences between supervised and unsupervised learning, explore how statistical modeling relates to machine learning, and analyze real-world examples of machine learning and its impact on society.
youWillLearn: Understand supervised and unsupervised machine learning concepts
Apply popular machine learning algorithms using Python
Build and evaluate classification and regression models
Work with clustering and dimensionality reduction techniques
Apply machine learning concepts through hands-on labs using Jupyter
overview: Machine learning plays a critical role in data science and artificial intelligence applications across industries. This course provides a practical introduction to machine learning concepts using Python.
You will explore commonly used algorithms including classification, regression, clustering, and dimensionality reduction, along with model evaluation techniques.
Through hands-on labs in a Jupyter environment, you will implement machine learning models, evaluate their performance, and understand real-world machine learning workflows.

modules.title: Module 2: Supervised Learning I
topics: Advantages and Disadvantages of Decision Trees

modules.title: Module 4: Unsupervised Learning
topics: Measuring Distances Between Clusters – Single Linkage

modules.title: Module 5: Dimensionality Reduction and Collaborative Filtering
topics: Dimensionality Reduction: Feature Extraction and Selection

syllabus.prerequisites: Python for data science is required. This course involves hands-on labs using Jupyter Notebook, a popular tool among data scientists. Learners should have working knowledge of Python for data analysis. If you are not comfortable with Python or Jupyter, it is recommended to complete introductory courses such as Data Analysis with Python or Data Science Hands-on with Open Source Tools before enrolling.

dataanalysiswithpython.name: Data Analysis with Python
dataanalysiswithpython.mainName: IBM Data Analysis with Python Certification

syllabus.atAGlance: Learn modern techniques of data analysis using Python and popular open-source libraries such as pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn to transform raw data into meaningful insights.
syllabus.intro: Data analysis is a highly in-demand and well-paid skill across industries. This course teaches you how to analyze data using Python, moving from Python fundamentals to working with diverse datasets. You will learn to prepare data for analysis, perform statistical analysis, create visualizations, build predictive models, and design data pipelines using open-source tools.
youWillLearn: Import datasets from multiple sources using Python
Clean and prepare data for analysis
Manipulate and analyze pandas DataFrames
Summarize and explore data using statistical techniques
Build machine learning models using scikit-learn
Create data pipelines for end-to-end analysis
overview: Python and open-source libraries have transformed the field of data analysis by replacing expensive and closed tools with flexible, powerful alternatives.
In this course, you will work with Pandas, NumPy, SciPy, and scikit-learn to load, clean, manipulate, analyze, and visualize real-world datasets.
Through lectures, hands-on labs, and assignments, you will gain practical experience in data analysis and predictive modeling using industry-standard tools.

modules.title: Module 1: Importing Datasets
topics: Importing and Exporting Data in Python

modules.title: Module 2: Cleaning and Preparing the Data

syllabus.prerequisites: Some prior Python experience is expected, along with basic knowledge of statistics. Completing a Python for Data Science course is recommended before enrolling. This course is self-paced and can be audited multiple times.

sqlandrelationaldatabase101.name: SQL and Relational Database 101
sqlandrelationaldatabase101.mainName: IBM SQL and Relational Database 101 Certification

syllabus.atAGlance: Are you ready to dive into the world of SQL and relational databases? In just a few hours, you will be able to discuss SQL basics and explain key aspects of the relational database model. Through hands-on exercises, this course guides you through your first steps into SQL and relational databases.
syllabus.intro: Data is one of the most critical assets of any business, and databases are essential for storing and processing data efficiently. SQL is the standard language used to interact with relational databases. In this introductory course, you will learn the fundamentals of SQL and relational databases, starting with relational model concepts and constraints. By the end of the course, you will have practiced the five basic SQL statements, explored advanced SQL syntax, and worked with join operations through hands-on exercises, assignments, and a final exam.
youWillLearn: Understand the fundamentals of SQL and relational databases
Explain relational data models, entities, and relationships
Create and manage database tables using SQL
Query, insert, update, and delete data using SQL statements
Work with advanced SQL concepts including joins and grouping
overview: Relational databases form the backbone of modern data-driven applications. This course provides a practical introduction to relational database concepts and SQL.
You will learn how data is modeled, stored, and retrieved using relational tables and how SQL is used to manipulate and query data.
The course includes lectures, hands-on exercises, assignments, review questions, and a final exam to reinforce learning and prepare you for real-world database usage.

modules.title: Module 1: SQL and Relational Databases 101
topics: Introduction to SQL and Relational Databases

modules.title: Module 2: Relational Model Constraints and Data Objects
Module 3: Data Definition Language (DDL) and Data Manipulation Language (DML)

syllabus.prerequisites: No prior experience is required. This beginner-level course is suitable for students, professionals, and anyone interested in learning SQL and relational database fundamentals.

syllabus.atAGlance: This beginner-level course is ideal for anyone looking to jumpstart a career in IT or a cybersecurity-related role, or for those who want to understand cybersecurity concepts to communicate effectively with IT security teams. The course introduces essential cybersecurity concepts, threats, and best practices needed to protect systems and data.
syllabus.intro: Cybersecurity presents ongoing challenges for organizations that rely on IT infrastructures and data. This course helps you build foundational cybersecurity skills to address common security threats and understand best practices for safeguarding systems, networks, and information. It serves as a first step toward roles such as cyber analyst or entry-level security engineer and is also valuable for professionals who work closely with IT security personnel.
youWillLearn: Recognize data security, integrity, and confidentiality protection needs
Identify common cybersecurity threats, attacks, and vulnerabilities
Understand core cybersecurity tools such as firewalls, antivirus software, and encryption
Apply best practices for authentication, authorization, and secure device usage
Develop safe browsing and electronic communication security habits
overview: Cybersecurity is critical for protecting organizational data and IT systems from evolving threats. This course provides an introduction to cybersecurity concepts, tools, and best practices.
You will explore different types of cybersecurity risks related to malware, phishing, social engineering, browsing, authentication, and privacy.
Through lectures, activities, case studies, and hands-on labs, you will gain practical exposure to real-world cybersecurity scenarios and defensive techniques.

modules.title: Module 1: Security Concerns
topics: Phishing, Social Engineering, and Other Attacks

modules.title: Module 2: Security Best Practices
topics: Hands-on Lab: Enforce Strong Password Policies
Security Threats: Access Control, Authorization, and Authentication
Security Threats: Validation and Device Usage

modules.title: Module 3: Security Concerns and Safe Browsing
topics: Hands-on Lab: Managing Browser Security and Privacy Settings

syllabus.prerequisites: Basic computer operating skills are recommended, including familiarity with using a web browser. No prior cybersecurity experience is required.

syllabus.atAGlance: Kickstart your business analysis journey by building the fundamental skills companies need to drive digital transformation, inform strategic decisions, and deliver measurable business value.
syllabus.intro: Get ready to begin your journey into the highly rewarding field of business analysis. In this course, you will build the foundational skills and knowledge required to succeed as a business analyst and understand how organizations leverage business analysis to achieve digital transformation and data-driven growth.
youWillLearn: Fundamental business analysis skills aligned with the BABOK Guide
Hands-on practical knowledge to prepare for ECBA certification
Process modeling, requirements gathering, and stakeholder engagement strategies
Core concepts of systems analysis, data analysis, and data visualization
Digital transformation strategies and the role of AI in business analysis
overview: Business analysis is ranked among the top 10 in-demand skills globally, as organizations increasingly rely on professionals who can bridge business needs with technological solutions.
This course introduces the core principles of business analysis, including roles, responsibilities, essential competencies, certifications, and career pathways in the domain.

Through real-world industry examples, hands-on labs, and practical exercises, you will explore gap analysis, digital strategy, technology evaluation, and AI-driven enhancements in business analysis practices.

modules.title: Module 1: Basics of Business Analysis
topics: Role of Business Analysis in Enterprises
Evolution and Importance of Business Analysis
Skills Required for a Business Analyst
How to Become a Business Analyst

modules.title: Module 2: Business Analysis Process
topics: Steps in the Business Analysis Process
Features of Common Business Analysis Tools

modules.title: Module 4: Final Project and Exam
topics: Project: Streamlining Hospital Operations through Business Analysis

syllabus.prerequisites: This course is designed for aspiring business analysts beginning their journey in the field. It is also suitable for working professionals seeking to strengthen and expand their business analysis skills. No prior business analysis experience is required.

syllabus.atAGlance: Explore the origins, evolution, and modern applications of data science. Discover how data science practitioners uncover insights, identify trends, and drive decision-making across industries.
syllabus.intro: This course introduces you to the world of data science by uncovering what the field truly involves. Through real stories from practitioners and practical discussions, you will gain a clear understanding of the roles, tools, and impact of data science in today’s data-driven economy. Formerly known as Data Science 101, this course serves as an ideal starting point for aspiring data professionals.
youWillLearn: What data science is and how the field has evolved
Different career paths that lead to data science
Daily responsibilities of data science professionals
Core tools and technologies such as Python, R, and cloud platforms
How data science is applied in business decision-making
Common terminology and mindset of data science professionals
overview: Data science has historical roots dating back to ancient civilizations, where data was used for planning and prediction. Today, it has evolved into a powerful interdisciplinary field combining statistics, programming, and domain expertise.
This course provides insight into the real-world responsibilities of data scientists, including regression analysis, business reporting, and analytical problem-solving.
You will explore business use cases, recruitment strategies, and practical applications of data science, while understanding how companies leverage analytics to create value.

modules.title: Module 1: Defining Data Science
topics: "Data Science: The Sexiest Job in the 21st Century"

modules.title: Module 2: What Do Data Science Professionals Do?
topics: A Day in the Life of a Data Scientist

modules.title: Module 3: Data Science in Business
topics: Getting Started with Data Science in Organizations

modules.title: Module 4: Use Cases for Data Science
Module 5: Data Science Professionals
topics: Common Terminology Used by Data Scientists
What Makes Someone a Data Scientist?

syllabus.prerequisites: No prior experience in data science or programming is required. This beginner-friendly course is self-paced and can be taken at any time.

datavisualizationwithpython.name: Data Visualization with Python
datavisualizationwithpython.mainName: IBM Data Visualization with Python Certification

syllabus.atAGlance: Learn how to transform raw data into compelling visual stories using powerful Python libraries. Master the art of creating charts, graphs, and geospatial maps to effectively communicate insights and trends.
syllabus.intro: Data visualization is the graphical representation of data to efficiently communicate insights to stakeholders, clients, and decision-makers. This course teaches you how to convert complex datasets into meaningful visual stories. By leveraging Python visualization libraries such as Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Folium, you will learn to design engaging, insightful, and aesthetically appealing graphics that drive better understanding and smarter decisions.
youWillLearn: Core principles and importance of data visualization
How to create and customize plots using Matplotlib
Techniques for building histograms, bar charts, area plots, and scatter plots
Advanced visualizations including waffle charts and word clouds
Using Seaborn for statistical and regression plots
Creating interactive geospatial maps using Folium
overview: Data visualization plays a critical role in interpreting both small and large datasets. It helps uncover patterns, trends, and insights that may not be immediately visible in raw data.
In this course, you will learn how to leverage Python visualization libraries including Matplotlib, Seaborn, and Folium to create impactful data visualizations.
By the end of the course, you will be able to transform complex datasets into clear, visually appealing representations that enhance storytelling and support data-driven decision-making.

modules.title: Module 5: Creating Maps and Visualizing Geospatial Data

syllabus.prerequisites: Prior completion of Python 101 and Data Analysis with Python is recommended. Basic familiarity with Python programming and data manipulation concepts will help you gain the most from this course.

deeplearningwithtensorflow.name: Deep Learning with TensorFlow
deeplearningwithtensorflow.mainName: IBM Deep Learning with TensorFlow Certification

syllabus.atAGlance: Learn how deep neural networks uncover hidden patterns in unlabeled and unstructured data such as images, audio, and text. Apply deep learning techniques using TensorFlow to solve real-world problems.
syllabus.intro: This course introduces deep learning concepts and demonstrates how TensorFlow can be used to build, train, and optimize neural networks. Unlike traditional shallow neural networks, deep learning models contain multiple hidden layers capable of discovering complex structures in large-scale unstructured data. You will explore TensorFlow fundamentals, mathematical operations using tensors, and the execution pipeline before advancing to deep architectures such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), and Autoencoders.
youWillLearn: Core concepts of deep learning and neural networks
Fundamentals of TensorFlow including tensors, operations, and computational graphs
Implementation of regression and classification models
Backpropagation and optimization techniques
Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for image-related tasks
Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) and LSTM for sequential data
Unsupervised learning methods including RBMs and Autoencoders
overview: Deep learning models extend traditional neural networks by incorporating multiple hidden layers, allowing them to extract meaningful patterns from unstructured data such as images, sound, and text.
TensorFlow, developed by Google, is a powerful open-source library designed for numerical computation and machine learning using data flow graphs and tensors.
Through practical examples including regression, classification, curve fitting, and optimization problems, you will learn how to build, train, and evaluate deep learning models using TensorFlow.
The course concludes with advanced deep learning architectures including CNNs, RNNs, Restricted Boltzmann Machines, and Autoencoders.
syllabus.prerequisites: Prior knowledge of basic Neural Networks and Python programming is recommended. Familiarity with linear algebra and fundamental machine learning concepts will be beneficial.

syllabus.atAGlance: Understand the principles of DevOps and learn how development and operations teams collaborate to accelerate software delivery through automation, continuous integration, and cloud-based deployment strategies.
syllabus.intro: DevOps (Development and Operations) is a modern software development methodology that integrates development and IT operations to shorten the system development life cycle while delivering high-quality features, updates, and fixes in alignment with business goals. This course introduces the core DevOps concepts, automation practices, monitoring strategies, and cloud-based implementations that enable organizations to build, test, and deploy software efficiently.
youWillLearn: Core principles and fundamentals of DevOps
How DevOps aligns development and operations teams
Automation in software build, testing, and deployment
Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) concepts
Real-world DevOps use cases and business impact
Introduction to implementing DevOps on IBM Cloud
overview: DevOps bridges the gap between software development and IT operations by fostering collaboration, automation, and continuous delivery practices.
This course explores how organizations use DevOps to improve deployment frequency, reduce failure rates, and accelerate innovation.
You will learn about DevOps lifecycle stages, event monitoring, infrastructure automation, and cloud-based DevOps environments including IBM Cloud.

modules.title: Module 2: DevOps Use Case
topics: Improving Collaboration Between Dev and Ops

modules.title: Module 4: Introduction to DevOps on IBM Cloud

syllabus.prerequisites: Basic understanding of software development concepts and IT operations is recommended. Familiarity with programming or system administration fundamentals will be helpful but is not mandatory.

cloudcomputing.name: Introduction to Cloud Computing
cloudcomputing.mainName: IBM Introduction to Cloud Computing Certification

syllabus.atAGlance: Build a strong foundation in cloud computing by understanding core concepts, service and deployment models, cloud architecture components, storage types, and emerging cloud trends shaping modern businesses.
syllabus.intro: This course introduces the fundamental concepts of cloud computing from both business and technical perspectives. You will explore the history and evolution of cloud computing, understand service and deployment models, examine cloud architecture components, and analyze emerging cloud technologies. By the end of this course, you will have the foundational knowledge required to confidently understand and discuss cloud computing in professional environments.
youWillLearn: Definition, characteristics, and evolution of cloud computing
Cloud service models: IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS
Cloud deployment models: Public, Private, and Hybrid
Core components of cloud architecture including virtualization, VMs, storage, networking, and containers
Different types of cloud storage solutions
Emerging trends such as Hybrid Multicloud, Serverless, Microservices, and Cloud Native applications
overview: Cloud computing has transformed how organizations build, deploy, and scale applications. This course provides a structured introduction to its essential concepts and architecture.

You will explore the business case for cloud adoption, review major cloud service providers, and understand how emerging technologies such as AI, IoT, Blockchain, and Analytics leverage cloud platforms.
The course also examines modern cloud-native practices including serverless computing, microservices architecture, and application modernization strategies.

modules.title: Module 1: Overview of Cloud Computing
topics: Definition and Essential Characteristics of Cloud Computing
History and Evolution of Cloud Computing
Key Cloud Service Providers and Their Services

modules.title: Module 2: Cloud Adoption and Emerging Technologies
topics: Emerging Technologies Supported by Cloud (AI, IoT, Blockchain, Analytics)

modules.title: Module 3: Cloud Computing Service and Deployment Models
topics: Deployment Models: Public Cloud, Private Cloud, Hybrid Cloud

modules.title: Module 4: Components of Cloud Computing
topics: Virtualization, Virtual Machines, and Bare Metal

modules.title: Module 6: Cloud Native and Emergent Cloud Trends

syllabus.prerequisites: No prior knowledge of cloud computing is required. This beginner-level course is designed for learners from both technical and non-technical backgrounds. Basic familiarity with IT concepts is helpful but not mandatory.

quantumcomputing.name: Introduction to Quantum Computing
quantumcomputing.mainName: IBM Introduction to Quantum Computing Certification

syllabus.atAGlance: Build a strong foundation in quantum computing by exploring qubits, superposition, entanglement, and quantum logic gates. Gain practical exposure to fundamental quantum concepts and algorithms in this beginner-friendly introduction.
syllabus.intro: This course introduces you to the fascinating world of quantum computing. You will explore core quantum principles such as superposition, entanglement, and uncertainty, and understand how quantum systems differ from classical computing models. Through interactive labs and conceptual demonstrations, you will gain hands-on insight into the mechanics of quantum behavior before testing your understanding in a final assessment.
youWillLearn: Fundamental principles of quantum computing
Understanding qubits and superposition
Concept of quantum entanglement
Quantum uncertainty and measurement
Basic quantum logic gates and introductory algorithms
Practical exposure through guided quantum labs
overview: Quantum computing leverages the principles of quantum mechanics to process information in fundamentally new ways compared to classical computers.
This beginner-level course introduces foundational quantum concepts through simplified explanations and interactive lab experiences.
Learners will explore how qubits behave, how superposition and entanglement enable powerful computational possibilities, and how quantum systems are measured and analyzed.

modules.title: Module 1: The Hall of Wonders
topics: Foundations of Quantum Mechanics in Computing

modules.title: Module 5: Return to the Hall of Wonders
Module 6: Final Assessment
topics: Show What You Know – Final Quiz

syllabus.prerequisites: This is a beginner-level course. No prior knowledge of quantum computing, physics, or programming is required.
syllabus.gradingScheme: The minimum passing mark for the course is 80%. The final exam accounts for 100% of the overall course grade.

syllabus.atAGlance: Learn Scala, a powerful programming language that combines object-oriented and functional programming paradigms. Gain the foundational skills required to use Scala for data science and big data applications, including Apache Spark.
syllabus.intro: Scala is a modern programming language that runs on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) and is fully compatible with Java. It uniquely blends object-oriented programming (OOP) and functional programming (FP), making it highly expressive and scalable. Widely adopted in the big data ecosystem, especially with Apache Spark, Scala has become a valuable skill for data developers and data scientists. This course provides a comprehensive introduction to Scala fundamentals, tooling, development workflows, and advanced language features to prepare you for real-world data science applications.
youWillLearn: Core syntax and fundamentals of the Scala programming language
Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) concepts in Scala
Functional Programming (FP) principles in Scala
Scala development tools and build processes
Using Scala in Big Data environments such as Apache Spark
Advanced Scala language features and best practices
overview: Scala combines the robustness of Java with modern functional programming capabilities, making it ideal for scalable and high-performance applications.
This course is designed for data developers and data scientists who want to confidently start programming in Scala for data science tasks.
You will gain a strong understanding of Scala fundamentals, development tooling, and advanced language constructs while preparing to apply Scala in big data ecosystems.

modules.title: Module 1: Introduction to Scala
topics: Overview of Scala and JVM Compatibility

modules.title: Module 4: Scala for Data Science and Big Data
topics: Introduction to Apache Spark with Scala

syllabus.prerequisites: Experience with Java (preferred), Python, or another object-oriented programming language is recommended. No prior knowledge of Scala or data science concepts is required.

syllabus.atAGlance: Build a strong foundation for a career in software engineering by exploring core principles, development processes, architecture, tools, and programming fundamentals used across the industry.
syllabus.intro: This beginner-friendly course introduces you to the dynamic and in-demand field of software engineering. You will explore the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC), development methodologies such as Agile, programming foundations, architecture and design principles, and modern development tools and stacks. Through expert insights and hands-on labs, you will gain practical exposure to the tools, technologies, terminology, and workflows used by professional software engineers.
youWillLearn: How the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) supports structured software delivery
Core software development methodologies including Agile
Fundamental programming concepts including loops and conditionals
Differences between compiled and interpreted languages
Software architecture, design principles, and deployment models
Development tools, IDEs, and software stacks used in modern environments
Technical and soft skills required for software engineering careers
Career pathways and job opportunities in software engineering
overview: Nearly every industry relies on proprietary software systems to drive innovation and business growth, making software engineering one of the most sought-after professions globally.
This course is designed for beginners, career switchers, and professionals who want to understand software engineering principles and industry practices.

Through real-world insights from experienced engineers and practical labs such as building a 'Hello World' program and exploring development environments, you will gain both conceptual understanding and foundational hands-on skills.

modules.title: Module 1: What is Software Engineering?
topics: Software Building Process and Associated Roles

modules.title: Module 2: Introduction to Software Development
topics: Overview of Web and Cloud Development
Hands-on Lab: Getting Started with an IDE

modules.title: Module 3: Introduction to Programming
topics: Hands-on Lab: Programming Fundamentals with Python

modules.title: Module 4: Software Architecture, Design, and Patterns

syllabus.prerequisites: Basic computer literacy is required. No prior programming or software development experience is necessary.

syllabus.atAGlance: Master modern prompt engineering techniques to improve the effectiveness, reliability, and precision of generative AI systems such as ChatGPT and large language models (LLMs).
syllabus.intro: This course introduces the principles and best practices of prompt engineering — the process of designing and refining prompts to guide generative AI models toward producing accurate and meaningful outputs. Designed for professionals, developers, executives, students, and AI enthusiasts, this course demystifies prompt engineering and provides practical techniques to unlock the full potential of large language models (LLMs). Through real-world examples and hands-on labs, you will learn how to craft structured prompts that enhance performance, relevance, and clarity in AI-generated responses.
youWillLearn: Fundamentals of prompt engineering and its importance in generative AI
Zero-shot and few-shot prompting techniques
Chain-of-Thought and Tree-of-Thought reasoning approaches
Interview Pattern prompting strategy
Best practices for writing clear and effective prompts
Using prompt engineering tools such as IBM watsonx Prompt Lab, Spellbook, and Dust
Hands-on optimization of prompts in AI lab environments
overview: Prompt engineering is a critical skill for effectively interacting with generative AI models and controlling their outputs.
This course explores structured prompting strategies that improve reliability, reduce hallucinations, and enhance response quality.
You will experiment with various prompting techniques and tools while learning directly from practitioners who apply these methods in real-world AI applications.

modules.title: Module 4: Prompt Engineering Tools
topics: Introduction to IBM watsonx Prompt Lab

modules.title: Module 5: Hands-On Labs and Practical Applications
topics: Creating Effective Prompts in AI Classroom

syllabus.prerequisites: No prior experience in AI or programming is required. This course is suitable for beginners as well as professionals looking to enhance their ability to effectively interact with generative AI tools.

syllabus.atAGlance: Build a strong foundation in computerized accounting with TallyPrime. Learn essential accounting concepts, GST compliance, voucher entry, inventory management, and business reporting using industry-standard Tally software.
syllabus.intro: Tally Essentials Level 1 introduces learners to the fundamentals of accounting and business management using TallyPrime. This beginner-friendly course covers core accounting principles, company creation, ledger management, GST transactions, inventory handling, and report generation. Designed for students, entrepreneurs, and accounting professionals, this course equips you with practical, job-ready skills required to manage business accounts efficiently.
youWillLearn: Fundamentals of accounting principles and terminology
Creating and managing companies in TallyPrime
Recording accounting vouchers and transactions
GST setup and compliance in Tally
Inventory management and stock handling
Generating financial statements and business reports
overview: TallyPrime is one of the most widely used accounting software solutions for small and medium-sized businesses.
This course provides hands-on exposure to recording transactions, managing ledgers, and maintaining statutory compliance such as GST.
Learners will gain practical experience in managing real-world business scenarios including sales, purchases, payments, receipts, and financial reporting.

modules.title: Module 1: Introduction to Accounting and TallyPrime

syllabus.prerequisites: Basic computer literacy is required. No prior accounting or Tally experience is necessary.

syllabus.atAGlance: Advance your accounting expertise with TallyPrime by mastering GST compliance, advanced inventory management, banking operations, payroll processing, and business reporting for real-world business scenarios.
syllabus.intro: Tally Essentials Level 2 builds upon foundational accounting knowledge and focuses on advanced features of TallyPrime used in professional accounting environments. This course equips learners with practical skills in GST return filing, advanced inventory control, payroll management, banking reconciliation, cost centers, and financial reporting. Designed for aspiring accountants, finance professionals, and business owners, this course prepares you to confidently handle real-world accounting operations.
youWillLearn: Advanced GST configuration and return preparation
Banking transactions and bank reconciliation process
Payroll setup and employee management
Cost centers and job costing techniques
Advanced inventory features and stock valuation methods
Generating MIS and analytical financial reports
overview: Tally Essentials Level 2 focuses on advanced accounting functionalities required in professional business environments.
You will gain hands-on experience in managing taxation, payroll, cost accounting, and business reporting using TallyPrime.
This course prepares learners to handle practical accounting scenarios including compliance management, salary processing, stock control, and financial analysis.

syllabus.atAGlance: Master advanced accounting, statutory compliance, auditing, and business optimization using TallyPrime. Gain professional-level expertise to manage complex financial operations and enterprise accounting scenarios.
syllabus.intro: Tally Essentials Level 3 is designed for learners who want to achieve advanced proficiency in TallyPrime and handle complex accounting, taxation, compliance, and auditing tasks. This course focuses on statutory compliance including TDS and GST returns, advanced financial reporting, data security, audit features, and multi-company management. It prepares learners for professional accounting roles and real-world business environments.

Learners gain hands-on exposure to enterprise-level accounting tasks such as financial analysis, internal controls, and secure data management.

syllabus.atAGlance: Become a job-ready accounting professional by mastering TallyPrime from fundamentals to advanced statutory compliance, payroll, taxation, audit, and financial reporting in real-world business environments.
syllabus.intro: Tally Essentials Comprehensive is a complete accounting and business management program designed to take learners from foundational accounting principles to advanced professional-level expertise in TallyPrime. This course covers company setup, voucher entry, GST compliance, payroll processing, banking reconciliation, cost accounting, TDS, audit features, and enterprise-level financial reporting. Ideal for students, commerce graduates, entrepreneurs, and aspiring accountants, this program equips you with the practical skills required to confidently manage business accounts and statutory compliance in real-world scenarios.

syllabus.prerequisites: Basic computer literacy is required. No prior accounting or Tally experience is necessary, although familiarity with basic accounting concepts will be beneficial.

syllabus.atAGlance: Master GST compliance using TallyPrime by learning configuration, transaction recording, return filing, reconciliation, and statutory reporting for real-world business scenarios.
syllabus.intro: GST Using TallyPrime is a practical, hands-on course designed to help learners understand and implement Goods and Services Tax (GST) compliance using TallyPrime. This course covers GST fundamentals, company configuration, recording GST transactions, input tax credit (ITC) management, and return filing processes including GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B. Ideal for accounting professionals, business owners, and students, this course prepares you to confidently manage GST operations in real-world environments.
youWillLearn: Fundamentals of GST structure and taxation rules
Configuring GST in TallyPrime
Recording sales and purchase transactions with GST
Managing Input Tax Credit (ITC)
Generating GST reports and returns (GSTR-1, GSTR-3B)
GST reconciliation and compliance management
overview: GST compliance is a critical requirement for businesses operating in India. This course focuses on practical GST implementation using TallyPrime.
Learners will gain hands-on experience in managing taxable transactions, generating statutory reports, and preparing GST returns.
The course emphasizes real-world scenarios including multi-rate taxation, reverse charge mechanism, and input tax credit reconciliation.

syllabus.atAGlance: Become a certified Tally Professional by mastering advanced accounting, GST, TDS, payroll, compliance, auditing, and financial reporting using TallyPrime for real-world business environments.
syllabus.intro: Tally Professional is an advanced, career-focused program designed to prepare learners for professional accounting and taxation roles. This course covers complete business accounting workflows in TallyPrime, including GST return filing, TDS compliance, payroll processing, banking reconciliation, cost accounting, audit tools, and financial analysis. Ideal for commerce graduates, aspiring accountants, finance professionals, and entrepreneurs, this program equips you with industry-ready skills required to manage enterprise-level accounting operations.

overview: Tally Professional prepares learners to handle complete business accounting cycles in real-world scenarios.
